subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the biochemical process of photosynthesis and its limiting factors
Understand the specialized vascular tissues: Xylem and Phloem
Explain the mechanism of water transport through transpiration pull and root pressure
Evaluate the process of asexual and sexual reproduction in plants
Investigate the role of stomata in gas exchange and transpiration
Compare different modes of nutrition: Autotrophic, Saprotrophic, and Parasitic
Understand the significance of seed dispersal and germination for species survival

lesson_plan.title: Photosynthesis: The Biochemical Factory
topics_covered: Role of Chlorophyll and solar energy conversion
Limiting factors: Light intensity, $CO_2$ concentration, and Temperature
teaching_strategy: Flowcharting the input-output of a leaf
Experimental demonstration: Necessity of Sunlight (Starch test)
Discussion on variegated leaves
student_activities: Perform the Iodine test on a leaf to detect starch
Sketch the cross-section of a leaf identifying chloroplasts

lesson_plan.title: Alternative Modes of Nutrition
topics_covered: Insectivorous plants: Pitcher plant and Venus Flytrap
teaching_strategy: Case study analysis of Nitrogen-deficient soil
Visual presentation of specialized plant adaptations
Discussion on why Insectivorous plants perform photosynthesis
student_activities: Observe bread mould under a magnifying glass
Categorize a list of plants into their respective nutritional modes
assessment: Short answer: 'The Nitrogen Cycle in Legumes'

lesson_plan.title: Transport System: Xylem and Phloem
topics_covered: Unidirectional transport in Xylem vs. Bidirectional in Phloem
teaching_strategy: Microscopic slide observation of stem cross-sections
Analogy: Plumbing system vs. Distribution network
Diagrammatic representation of sap flow
student_activities: Observe dye movement in a Balsam plant stem

lesson_plan.title: Transpiration and Ascent of Sap
topics_covered: Mechanism of Stomatal opening and closing
teaching_strategy: Bell jar experiment to show water vapor release
Mathematical logic: How water reaches tall trees without a pump
Discussion on Wilting
student_activities: Prepare a leaf peel mount to observe stomata under a microscope
Measure transpiration rates using a simple potometer (Demonstration)
assessment: Reasoning: 'Why is transpiration a necessary evil?'

lesson_plan.title: Asexual Reproduction in Plants
topics_covered: Budding in Yeast and Fragmentation in Spirogyra
Spore formation in Ferns and Mosses
teaching_strategy: Hands-on activity: Potato eyes and Ginger rhizomes
Video analysis of Spirogyra fragmentation
Discussion on the advantages of clones
student_activities: Identify vegetative parts in common kitchen vegetables
Draw the stages of budding in Yeast

lesson_plan.title: Sexual Reproduction: Flowers and Seeds
topics_covered: Pollination: Self vs. Cross (Agents of pollination)
teaching_strategy: Flower dissection lab (Hibiscus/Mustard)
Tracing the pollen tube journey
Visualizing the transformation of Ovary to Fruit
student_activities: Dissect a flower and label Stamen and Pistil
assessment: Sequence chart: Pollination to Seed formation

lesson_plan.title: Seed Dispersal and Mastery Review
topics_covered: Dispersal by Wind, Water, Animals, and Explosion
Significance of dispersal in avoiding competition
teaching_strategy: Sample analysis of different seeds (Wings, hooks, etc.)
Review of Assertion-Reasoning questions
Final doubt clearing session
student_activities: Match seeds to their dispersal agents based on morphology
Complete the Chapter Mastery Unit Test
assessment: Evaluation of logical deduction in 'Seed Strategy' project

teaching_aids: Microscope and slides (Stomata, Xylem, Yeast budding)
Balsam plant, Hibiscus flowers, Potato/Ginger samples
Iodine solution and Alcohol for starch test
teacher_tip: For Class 7, the concept of 'Suction Pull' is often hard to grasp. Use the analogy of drinking through a straw—as water evaporates from the leaves (sucking), it pulls the entire column of water up from the roots. Ensure safety during the starch test when heating alcohol.
